content.claim: Since 2013, central government has assumed responsibility for public school maintenance through the Public Education Act, transferring authority from municipalities to the state and consolidating schools under KLIK, which has curtailed institutional autonomy over hiring and budgets and reduced local capacity-planning flexibility.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: The institutional effects of centralization on 6-/8-year track network resilience are inferred rather than directly measured in available sources
No audit of specific capacity-planning delays or failures attributable to centralization is provided
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
